text: Iliotibial band syndrome is caused by excessive tension in the ITB leading to compression and inflammation of tissues beneath it on the lateral aspect of the knee.
ITBS may be promoted by anatomical predisposition, joint malalignments, aberrant activation of inserting muscles, and excessive ITB stiffness.
Hip abductor strengthening may correct excessive hip adduction in ITBS patients but may also increase ITB strain, requiring careful programming.
Intermittent stretching interventions are unlikely to change the ITB's length or mechanical properties due to the band's inherent stiffness.
Running gait retraining is a promising intervention for ITBS but remains understudied in controlled trials.
ITBS has high symptom recurrence rates with standard conservative treatment, suggesting current approaches may not address causal factors.
